docs.title: Technical Writing Portfolio
docs.text:
Hi, I'm David Posto, a technical writer with experience creating knowledge base articles, SOPs, troubleshooting guides, and end-user support documentation for IT service desks and healthcare workflows.

This site is a collection of writing samples that demonstrate my documentation style: clear step-by-step procedures, consistent structure, and plain language designed for front-line support staff.

docs.title: What You'll Find Here
docs.text:
This portfolio contains sample documentation demonstrating:

  • Knowledge base article development
  • Step-by-step troubleshooting procedures
  • Process and workflow documentation
  • Decision-tree based support guides
  • Documentation written for front-line support teams

docs.title: About These Articles
docs.text:
These articles are based on real help desk workflows I've documented professionally. Names, locations, and organization-specific details have been generalized to protect confidentiality.

Each article follows a structured format:

  • Symptoms — what the caller or user is experiencing
  • Solution — step-by-step procedures with decision branches
  • Escalation — what to do when standard steps don't resolve the issue

docs.title: New Patient Scheduling
docs.text:
Healthcare workflow documentation describing the process for scheduling new patients, including decision points, insurance verification, provider selection, and escalation paths.

Read Article

docs.title: ISP Static Configuration
docs.text:
IT networking documentation describing the process for verifying and reconfiguring a back office machine to test ISP static IP settings, including adapter configuration, direct ISP device testing, and escalation paths.

Read Article

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I'm based in the Indianapolis area and am currently seeking Technical Writer, Knowledge Management, and IT Documentation opportunities.

  • GitHub: github.com/dposto

docs.title: Verifying ISP Static IP Configuration Using a Back Office Workstation
docs.text:
This article provides procedures for verifying whether a site's ISP-provided static IP configuration is correctly configured and reachable by directly connecting a workstation to the ISP equipment.

Author David Posto Document Type Knowledge Base Article Audience IT Support Agents Portfolio Version June 2026

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
Use this procedure when troubleshooting a site that may have an ISP-side static IP configuration issue.

This process may be required when:

  1. The site is operating on a backup internet connection.
  2. The site is connected through a secondary WAN interface.
  3. The site is completely offline.
  4. Connectivity issues suggest a static IP configuration problem.

docs.title: Pre-Step 1: Set Priority
docs.text: 1. Set the Service Order priority to Priority 1.

docs.title: Pre-Step 2: Verify Eligibility
docs.text:
1. Verify one of the following conditions exists:

  2. The site is already operating on a backup internet connection.

  3. The site is completely offline.

Note: In many environments, a secondary WAN interface is configured using DHCP and can be used for temporary connectivity testing.

docs.title: Pre-Step 3: Verify ISP Static IP Information
docs.text:
1. Ask the caller:

Do you have the site's static IP information from the ISP?

  1. Record the following information:

  2. Static IP Address

  3. Subnet Mask

  4. Default Gateway

  5. If the caller does not have the information:

  6. Inform the caller:

    You'll need to contact your ISP and request your static IP address, subnet mask, and default gateway information.

  7. Document the interaction.

  8. Close the Service Order.

docs.title: Step 1: Verify Secondary WAN Connectivity
docs.text:
1. Confirm the following:

  2. The ISP modem or router is connected to the secondary WAN interface.

  3. The secondary WAN connection is active.

  4. Have the caller perform an internet search to verify connectivity.

  5. If connectivity succeeds:

  6. Document successful connectivity.

  7. Proceed to Step 2.

  8. If connectivity fails:

  9. Recommend a temporary backup internet solution if appropriate.

  10. Document findings.
  11. Close the Service Order.

docs.title: Step 2: Identify the Workstation
docs.text:
1. Determine which workstation will be used for testing.

  2. If necessary:

  3. Identify the workstation's network connection.

  4. Verify the network port currently in use.
  5. Document the connection details.

  6. Ensure the workstation can be restored to its original state after testing.

  7. Proceed to Step 3.

docs.title: Step 3: Establish Remote Access
docs.text: 1. Initiate a screen-sharing session with the workstation if available.

docs.title: Step 4: Configure the Network Adapter
docs.text:
1. Open:

  2. View Network Connections

  3. Ethernet Adapter Properties

  4. Internet Protocol Version 4 (IPv4)

  5. Document the current configuration:

  6. IP Address

  7. Subnet Mask
  8. Default Gateway
  9. Preferred DNS Server

  10. Alternate DNS Server

  11. Update the adapter using the ISP-provided static IP information.

  12. Configure DNS servers as appropriate for the environment.

  13. Save the changes.

docs.title: Step 5: Connect the Workstation Directly to the ISP Device
docs.text:
1. Determine whether the ISP device is:

  2. An Optical Network Terminal (ONT)

  3. A modem or router with a single Ethernet port

  4. A modem or router with multiple available Ethernet ports

  5. Connect the workstation directly to the ISP equipment using the appropriate method.

⚠️ Warning: This process may temporarily interrupt network connectivity for the site.

docs.title: Step 6: Test Internet Access
docs.text:
1. Have the caller browse to:

https://speedtest.net

  1. If the test is successful:

  2. Record:

    1. Download Speed
    2. Upload Speed
    3. Proceed to Step 7.

  3. If the test is unsuccessful:

  4. Record:

    1. Exact error message
    2. Relevant troubleshooting observations
    3. Proceed to Step 7.

docs.title: Step 7: Restore Original Adapter Configuration
docs.text:
1. Return the network adapter settings to the values documented in Step 4.

  2. Save the changes.

docs.title: Step 8: Restore Network Connections
docs.text:
1. If any network connections were disconnected during testing:

  2. Reconnect the workstation to its original network connection.

  3. Reconnect the secondary WAN connection if applicable.
  4. Verify that network services have recovered.

docs.title: Step 9: Verify Business-Critical Applications
docs.text:
1. Confirm connectivity to business-critical applications and services.

  2. Document the results.

  3. If connectivity issues remain:

  4. Record the current public IP address.

  5. Escalate according to established procedures.

docs.title: Step 10: Escalation
docs.text:
1. If the issue remains unresolved:

  2. Document all troubleshooting performed.

  3. Include relevant test results and observations.
  4. Assign the Service Order according to established escalation procedures.

docs.title: New Patient Scheduling
docs.text:
This article provides procedures for scheduling new primary care patients, including insurance verification, provider selection, appointment scheduling, and escalation scenarios.

Author David Posto Document Type Knowledge Base Article Audience Scheduling Agents Last Updated June 2026

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text: A new patient needs to schedule an appointment with a Primary Care Provider (PCP).

docs.title: Pre-Step 1: Cardiac Concerns or Shortness of Breath
docs.text:
1. If the patient expresses concern about cardiac issues or shortness of breath at any time:

  2. Inform the caller:

    Your symptoms suggest a serious condition that requires immediate medical attention. Please hang up the phone and call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room.

  3. Close the Service Order.

docs.title: Pre-Step 2: Addressing Sensitive Topics
docs.text: 1. For information on addressing sensitive topics during calls, such as gender or diagnosis, refer to the [Sensitive Topics Procedure] article.

docs.title: Step 1: Determine if New or Existing Patient
docs.text:
1. Ask the caller:

Are you needing to schedule an appointment with a new primary care provider?

  1. If Yes:

  2. Proceed to Step 2.

  3. If the caller is an existing patient:

  4. Inform the caller:

    This number is for scheduling new patients. Are you interested in scheduling with a new provider, or would you like to stay with your current provider?

  5. If the patient wants to stay with their current provider:

    1. Note in the Service Order that the caller is not a new patient.
    2. If the practice is open, warm transfer the caller. See [Warm Transfer Procedures].
    3. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

  6. If the patient would like to schedule with a new provider:

    1. Proceed to Step 2.

docs.title: Step 2: Gather Patient's Insurance and Determine if Accepted
docs.text:
1. Refer to the [Accepted Insurance Reference] to determine whether the caller's insurance is accepted.

  2. Note the caller's insurance in the Service Order.

  3. If the insurance is accepted:

  4. Inform the patient:

    Your insurance is accepted.

  5. Proceed to Step 3.

  6. If the insurance is not accepted:

  7. Ask the caller:

    Your insurance is not currently accepted. Would you still like to schedule a new appointment?

  8. If Yes:

    1. Proceed to Step 3.

  9. If No:

    1. Inform the patient:

      Thank you for calling. Have a great day.

    2. Close the Service Order.

  10. If the patient does not have insurance:

  11. Proceed to Step 3.

docs.title: Step 3: Determine Which Location the Patient Needs to Schedule In
docs.text:
1. Inform the caller of the available service area locations.

  2. Ask the caller:

Which area do you live closest to, or where would you prefer to see the provider?

  1. Note the caller's response in the Service Order.

docs.title: Step 4: Determine Which Practice to Schedule With
docs.text:
1. Open the [PCPs Taking New Patients] scheduling reference.

  2. Navigate to the PCPs Taking New Patients tab.

  3. Locate the city or town noted in Step 3.

  4. Review the available practices and provider locations.

  5. Present the options to the caller and confirm which practice they prefer.

⚠️ Note: A practice must have providers listed beneath it to be eligible for new patient scheduling.

  1. If you are unable to determine the correct practice:

  2. During standard business hours, contact the [Scheduling Supervisor].

  3. Outside business hours, or if the supervisor is unavailable, proceed to Step 8.

  4. Note the selected practice in the Service Order.

docs.title: Step 5: Determine Which Provider to Schedule With
docs.text:
1. If the caller requests a specific provider, attempt to schedule with that provider first.

  2. Review the providers listed under the practice selected in Step 4.

  3. If more than one provider is listed:

  4. Check the Scheduling Priority column.

  5. If a provider is flagged, select that provider first.

  6. If no priority is listed, any available provider may be selected.

  7. Verify that the patient's age and insurance are accepted by the selected provider.

  8. Review any notes listed in the Special Instructions column.

  9. Communicate relevant instructions to the caller.

  10. If requested, provide provider information using the available biography link.

  11. Note the selected provider in the Service Order.

  12. Proceed to Step 6.

docs.title: Step 6: Select an Appointment with the Provider
docs.text:
1. Click the Online Scheduling link for the selected provider.

  2. If the message "Sorry, we couldn't find any open appointments." appears:

  3. Repeat Step 5 and check for additional providers at the same practice.

  4. If no additional providers are available:

    1. Ask the caller:

      Would you like to schedule an appointment at a different location?

    2. If Yes:

      1. Repeat Steps 4–5 for an alternate practice.

    3. If No:

      1. Note the preferred practice, desired dates and times, and any issues in the Service Order.
      2. Warm transfer if the office is open.
      3. If closed, proceed to Step 8.

  5. Review available appointments with the caller.

  6. If "more..." appears beneath a time slot:

  7. Hover over it to reveal additional appointment times.

  8. If no appointment is available within 7 days:

  9. Repeat Step 5 and check for additional providers at the same practice.

  10. If no providers have availability within 7 days:

    1. Inform the caller of the next available appointment.

    2. If the appointment is acceptable:

      1. Select the appointment.
      2. Proceed to Step 7.

    3. If the appointment is not acceptable:

      1. Ask the caller:

        Would you like to schedule at a different location?

      2. If Yes:

        1. Repeat Steps 4–5 for an alternate practice.

      3. If No:

        1. If the next available appointment is more than 2 weeks away:

        2. Ask the caller:

          Would you like to schedule that appointment and be added to a waitlist in case an earlier opening becomes available? If a sooner appointment opens up, you'll receive a text notification to your cell phone.

        3. If Yes:

          1. Note SMS Waitlist Opt-In and the caller's cell phone number in the Service Order.
          2. Proceed to Step 7.

        4. If No:

          1. Note the preferred practice and desired dates/times.
          2. Warm transfer if open or proceed to Step 8.

        5. If the next available appointment is less than 2 weeks away:

        6. Note the preferred practice and desired dates/times.

        7. Warm transfer if open or proceed to Step 8.

  11. Proceed to Step 7.

docs.title: Step 7: Gather Patient Information and Schedule Appointment
docs.text:
1. Click the selected appointment.

  2. Complete the CAPTCHA.

  3. Verify:

  4. Provider

  5. Appointment date
  6. Appointment time

  7. Location

  8. In the Reason for Visit field, enter the reason for the appointment.

  9. Click Continue.

  10. Under Continue as a Guest, click Continue.

docs.title: Patient Information Screen
docs.text:
1. Ask for and enter the patient's information.

  2. Complete all required fields.

🚫 Do NOT request the patient's Social Security Number. If the SSN field is required, enter 000-00-0000 or 999-99-9999.

  1. Click Next.

docs.title: Insurance Information Screen
docs.text:
1. Ask for and enter the patient's insurance information.

  2. If the patient has no insurance:

  3. Select No Insurance.

  4. If the insurance is not listed:

  5. Select Not Listed.

  6. Complete all required fields.

💡 FYI: Subscriber ID may also appear as Subscriber Number, Member Number, Member ID, Policy Number, or Policy ID.

  1. Click Schedule It!

  2. Inform the caller:

You will shortly receive an email confirmation of your appointment, as well as an email with a link to register for the patient portal.

  1. If the caller's insurance was not listed:

  2. Provide the office phone number.

  3. Offer a warm transfer if the caller would like additional assistance.

docs.title: If the Appointment is Successfully Scheduled
docs.text:
1. Note the following in the Service Order:

  2. Appointment Scheduled

  3. Appointment Date
  4. Appointment Time

  5. Appointment Location

  6. Close the Service Order.

docs.title: If Unable to Schedule the Appointment
docs.text:
1. Note the following in the Service Order:

  2. Practice required

  3. Preferred dates and times
  4. Errors encountered

  5. Additional troubleshooting notes

  6. Warm transfer if the office is open.

  7. If the office is closed, proceed to Step 8.

docs.title: Step 8: Escalation
docs.text:
1. If the issue remains unresolved:

  2. Follow the appropriate escalation procedure.

  3. Refer to the [Escalation Procedures] article.
